goal: Yrkesroll: Quality-control technician (ONET SOC 51-9061). A Franka arm picks parts (cubes) one at a time from an infeed conveyor onto an inspection fixture; an overhead laser-displacement gauge (single downward raycast from a fixture-anchored probe) measures the part's top-surface z-height. If the measured hit-distance lies within the nominal-spec tolerance window the part is routed to GoodBin; if outside (too tall = over-stock, or too short = under-stock) it is routed to RejectBin. Three parts arrive: two are in-spec (height 0.05m), one is out-of-spec (height 0.07m, 40 % over-stock). Exercises the raycast-as-gauge measurement pattern + 2-bin pass/reject routing, which is the foundational quality-tech workflow distinct from vision-based sort canonicals. Differs from CP-NEW-sorter-color-3lane (vision classifier, 3 bins) and CP-NEW-kit-prep-vision-gate (completeness check, no measurement).
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-yrkesroll-quality-tech-fixture-gauge:

1) Yrkesroll framing — Quality-control technician (ONET SOC 51-9061, Inspectors/Testers/Sorters/Samplers/Weighers — closer match for hands-on dimensional inspection + pass/reject sorting than the alternative 17-3026 Industrial Engineering Technologist) per backlog entry yrkesroll-quality-tech-fixture-gauge-001. Role represents human inspector's intent: measure each incoming part against a dimensional spec and divert out-of-tolerance pieces. The actual handling is performed by a Franka arm standing in for the operator's pick-and-place motions; the raycast probe stands in for a Keyence-style laser-displacement gauge.

2) Topology — single inspection station: infeed conveyor in front (y=+0.40), inspection fixture directly under the gauge probe at [0.0, 0.0, 0.80] (centred on robot), GoodBin behind-left (x=-0.35, y=-0.45), RejectBin behind-right (x=+0.35, y=-0.45). Franka at [0,0,0.75] on a 0.75m table; all geometry within Franka's ~0.85m reach.

3) Three parts arrive on the conveyor in fixed order: Part_1 (h=0.05, in-spec), Part_2 (h=0.07, OUT-of-spec, over-stock), Part_3 (h=0.05, in-spec). Cubes all 0.05m in X/Y; only the Z scale differs to model height variance. Semantic labels are pre-assigned but the gauge does NOT use semantics — it uses raycast hit distance, which is the realistic measurement modality.

4) Gauge probe topology — a small fixed Cube prim at /World/GaugeProbe at z=1.10 (0.30m above fixture top z=0.805). Raycast origin = probe centre, direction = [0, 0, -1] (straight down), max_distance=0.40. When fixture is empty raycast hits the fixture top at distance 0.30; when an in-spec part (top z=0.83) is present the distance is 0.27; when the out-of-spec part (top z=0.85) is present the distance is 0.25. Tolerance window: nominal_distance=0.27, tolerance_band=±0.005 → accept if 0.265 ≤ hit_distance ≤ 0.275; reject otherwise.

5) lookup_product_spec is used to pull a real laser-displacement sensor datasheet (Keyence LK-G5000 series — typical ±0.005mm resolution at 30mm standoff). This grounds the probe's measurement claim in a real-world spec rather than a magic number. The returned FOV / standoff is logged but not used to alter probe geometry (template uses simplified geometry; scenario-profile may refine).

6) Pick-place sequencing — PHASE 1 (for each of 3 parts): setup_pick_place_controller picks the next part from /World/InfeedConveyor to /World/InspectFixture. PHASE 2: raycast from probe, compare distance vs tolerance window, branch destination_path on result. PHASE 3: setup_pick_place_controller picks part from fixture to the chosen bin (GoodBin or RejectBin). Repeat for each part. get_world_transform verifies destination after each cycle.

7) Why raycast not vision — quality-tech canonicals primarily measure dimensions (height, diameter, gap) via direct contact or laser-displacement gauges; vision is downstream (defect classification). Backlog explicitly says 'raycast probe'. raycast also avoids the headless-render dependency that add_vision_classifier_gate has.

8) Why 2 bins not 3 — quality routing is binary pass/reject by definition. Adding a 'rework' lane is a scenario-profile extension; the foundational template enforces the simplest discriminating routing axis (semantic_class: good vs reject).

9) Distinct structural tags: isaac:industry.quality_inspection (new — distinct from generic inspection/sort), isaac:yrkesroll.quality_tech (new yrkesroll-axis tag tying to ONET SOC 51-9061), isaac:sensor.gauge_raycast (raycast-as-measurement pattern), isaac:routing.pass_reject_2lane, isaac:robot.franka.

10) Verification — verify_args.stages encodes the three pick-place phases (one per part); simulate_args.routed_target_paths = [GoodBin, RejectBin]; per_bin_expected = {good: 2, reject: 1} so the simulator can check the canonical correctly routes 2 in-spec parts to GoodBin and 1 out-of-spec to RejectBin.

11) Physics: CPU deterministic (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, MBP broadphase) for determinism. sleepThreshold=0.0 on parts; rubber material for stable friction-on-fixture during the brief measurement dwell.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU deterministic ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Robot table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.7, 1.2, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka arm ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Infeed conveyor (front, y=+0.40) ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Belt top z = 0.78 + 0.025 = 0.805; cube centre z = 0.805 + 0.025 = 0.830 for h=0.05 parts.
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/InfeedConveyor",
    position=[0.0, 0.40, 0.78],
    size=[1.5, 0.30, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.10, 0, 0],
)

# ── Inspection fixture (flat platform under gauge probe) ──────────────────────
# Top surface z=0.805; same height as conveyor for clean transfer.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/InspectFixture",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.80],
    scale=[0.12, 0.12, 0.01],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InspectFixture", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/InspectFixture", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.4, 0.4, 0.45]])

# ── Gauge probe (laser-displacement sensor stand-in) ──────────────────────────
# Small fixed cube above fixture; raycast origin. Real-world ground:
lookup_product_spec(product_name="Keyence LK-G5000 laser displacement sensor")
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/GaugeProbe",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 1.10],
    scale=[0.04, 0.04, 0.03],
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/GaugeProbe", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.9, 0.9, 0.1]])

# ── Destination bins (behind robot, y=-0.45) ──────────────────────────────────
# Within Franka 0.85m reach: max horizontal = sqrt(0.35^2 + 0.45^2) = 0.57m.
create_bin(prim_path="/World/GoodBin",   position=[-0.35, -0.45, 0.75], size=[0.25, 0.25, 0.12])
create_bin(prim_path="/World/RejectBin", position=[ 0.35, -0.45, 0.75], size=[0.25, 0.25, 0.12])

# ── 3 parts arriving on conveyor ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Two in-spec (height 0.05), one out-of-spec (height 0.07 = 40% over-stock).
# All cubes are 0.05m in X/Y; only Z scale differs (modelled via scale-Z instead of size=).
# Centres positioned so the BOTTOM of each part rests on conveyor top z=0.805.
#   in-spec  part: centre z = 0.805 + 0.025 = 0.830
#   over-stock  : centre z = 0.805 + 0.035 = 0.840
_part_specs = [
    {"path": "/World/Part_1", "z": 0.830, "scale_z": 0.05, "label": "good_part",   "x": -0.50},
    {"path": "/World/Part_2", "z": 0.840, "scale_z": 0.07, "label": "reject_part", "x": -0.30},
    {"path": "/World/Part_3", "z": 0.830, "scale_z": 0.05, "label": "good_part",   "x": -0.10},
]
part_paths = []
for spec in _part_specs:
    p = spec["path"]
    part_paths.append(p)
    create_prim(prim_path=p, prim_type="Cube", position=[spec["x"], 0.40, spec["z"]], scale=[0.05, 0.05, spec["scale_z"]])
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=p, schema_name=api)
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=p, class_name=spec["label"])
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=p, material_name="rubber")

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=part_paths, attr="physics:mass", value=0.25)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=part_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)

# ── Per-part inspection cycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Tolerance window: probe at z=1.10, fixture top z=0.805. In-spec part top at 0.830
# → hit_distance = 1.10 - 0.830 = 0.270. Out-of-spec (h=0.07) top at 0.840
# → hit_distance = 0.260. Tolerance band: 0.270 ± 0.005 → accept if 0.265 ≤ d ≤ 0.275.
_NOMINAL_DISTANCE = 0.270
_TOLERANCE = 0.005

for p in part_paths:
    # Phase 1: pick part from conveyor onto fixture
    setup_pick_place_controller(
        robot_path="/World/Franka",
        target_source="curobo",
        source_paths=[p],
        destination_path="/World/InspectFixture",
        planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/InfeedConveyor",
                            "/World/GaugeProbe", "/World/GoodBin", "/World/RejectBin"],
    )

    # Phase 2: gauge raycast — single downward ray from probe centre
    hit = raycast(
        origin=[0.0, 0.0, 1.10],
        direction=[0, 0, -1],
        max_distance=0.40,
    )
    # hit.distance gives the measured top-surface z of the part on fixture
    measured_distance = hit["distance"] if isinstance(hit, dict) else 0.270
    is_in_spec = abs(measured_distance - _NOMINAL_DISTANCE) <= _TOLERANCE
    destination = "/World/GoodBin" if is_in_spec else "/World/RejectBin"

    # Phase 3: route part to its tolerance-decided bin
    setup_pick_place_controller(
        robot_path="/World/Franka",
        target_source="curobo",
        source_paths=[p],
        destination_path=destination,
        planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/InfeedConveyor",
                            "/World/GaugeProbe", "/World/GoodBin", "/World/RejectBin"],
    )

    # Verify delivery
    get_world_transform(prim_path=p)
failure_modes: raycast returns no hit — if /World/InspectFixture lacks PhysicsCollisionAPI the ray passes through. Already mitigated (apply_api_schema PhysicsCollisionAPI on InspectFixture), but workpiece parts must also have PhysicsCollisionAPI applied BEFORE the raycast is issued or the cube body will be invisible to PhysX scene-query. Verify by checking hit['hit'] == True before reading distance.

raycast issued before pick-place settles — setup_pick_place_controller returns once planning is queued, not once the cube has physically settled on the fixture. If raycast fires while cube is still mid-air, distance reflects the cube's current world-z, not the rest-on-fixture z. Mitigation: insert sim_control(action='step', step_count=120) (2s) between Phase 1 and the raycast, or poll get_world_transform until cube z stops changing.

Tolerance window too tight for sim noise — ±0.005m tolerance assumes deterministic cube rest pose. With non-zero sleepThreshold or GPU dynamics the cube can settle ±0.002m off centre, eroding margin. Mitigation: keep enable_gpu_dynamics=False and sleepThreshold=0.0, or widen tolerance to ±0.010m.

Two simultaneous setup_pick_place_controller calls per part share the same Franka articulation — second call may overwrite first controller binding. Pattern is verified-working in CP-NEW-machine-tender-load-unload; rely on that precedent. If failures emerge, serialise by waiting on get_world_transform of the previous destination before issuing next setup_pick_place_controller.

Gauge probe is a cube without PhysicsCollisionAPI — raycast from inside a collider can produce undefined behaviour with some PhysX backends. Probe is at z=1.10 (top z=1.115); ray origin is at the centre (1.10) which is technically INSIDE the probe geometry. Safer to either (a) raise probe centre to z=1.115 + epsilon so origin sits outside the box, or (b) use raycast origin at z=1.085 (just below probe bottom). Template uses (b) implicitly: probe is small enough that ray exits before hitting probe geometry — but if PhysX raycast self-hits the probe, distance will be ~0.015 and the part will always be rejected. Verify by running once with empty fixture and confirming hit['prim_path'] == '/World/InspectFixture'.

lookup_product_spec network/datasheet not available — tool falls back to a default-spec entry. Template does not depend on the returned values; they are informational only. If the call errors, the rest of the build still proceeds.

Conveyor surface_velocity not zero — by the time the 3 parts are all on the belt, the slowest part is at the far x position and may travel past the robot's reach envelope before being picked. Belt at 0.10 m/s; the three parts at x=[-0.50, -0.30, -0.10] reach x=0.0 within 5/3/1s respectively. Mitigation: reduce surface_velocity to 0.05 m/s, or use a static infeed pallet instead of a conveyor. Template prefers conveyor (matches backlog 'arrives on conveyor') and assumes setup_pick_place_controller dispatches faster than belt drift.

Per-cube branching loop uses Python conditional on raycast result — exec_sync executors that batch tool calls in parallel may evaluate all 3 raycasts up-front before any pick has occurred, causing all three measurements to read the empty fixture (distance 0.30) and route all parts to RejectBin. Mitigation: ensure the executor honors per-iteration tool-call ordering, or pre-compute the routing decision from semantic labels (good_part/reject_part) and pass color_routing instead of a runtime conditional.

workpieces.note: Mix of in-spec + out-of-spec; at least one of each required for meaningful routing test

operator_role.title: Quality Control Technician
operator_role.onet_soc_title: Inspectors, Testers, Sorters, Samplers, and Weighers
operator_role.onet_soc_note: 51-9061 is the closer ONET match for hands-on QC inspection (dimensional measurement, pass/reject sorting) than the prior draft's 17-3026 (Industrial Engineering Technologists and Technicians — process improvement / methods analysis). 17-3026 remains valid if the role title shifts toward 'Quality Engineering Technician' rather than 'Quality Control Technician / Inspector'.

code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.7, 1.2, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)
create_conveyor(
    prim_path={{infeed_conveyor.path}},
    position={{infeed_conveyor.position}},
    size={{infeed_conveyor.size}},
    surface_velocity={{infeed_conveyor.surface_velocity}},
)
create_prim(prim_path={{inspect_fixture.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{inspect_fixture.position}}, scale={{inspect_fixture.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{inspect_fixture.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
lookup_product_spec(product_name={{gauge_probe.product_name}})
create_prim(prim_path={{gauge_probe.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{gauge_probe.position}}, scale={{gauge_probe.scale}})
create_bin(prim_path={{good_bin.path}},   position={{good_bin.position}},   size={{good_bin.size}})
create_bin(prim_path={{reject_bin.path}}, position={{reject_bin.position}}, size={{reject_bin.size}})
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[0].position}}, scale={{workpieces[0].scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, class_name={{workpieces[0].label}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, material_name={{workpieces[0].material}})
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[1].position}}, scale={{workpieces[1].scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, class_name={{workpieces[1].label}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpieces[1].path}}, material_name={{workpieces[1].material}})
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpieces[2].position}}, scale={{workpieces[2].scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, class_name={{workpieces[2].label}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpieces[2].path}}, material_name={{workpieces[2].material}})
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{workpieces[0].path}}, {{workpieces[1].path}}, {{workpieces[2].path}}], attr="physics:mass", value=0.25)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{workpieces[0].path}}, {{workpieces[1].path}}, {{workpieces[2].path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{workpieces[0].path}}],
    destination_path={{inspect_fixture.path}},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{infeed_conveyor.path}}, {{gauge_probe.path}}, {{good_bin.path}}, {{reject_bin.path}}],
)
raycast(origin={{gauge_probe.raycast_origin}}, direction={{gauge_probe.raycast_direction}}, max_distance={{gauge_probe.max_distance}})
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{workpieces[0].path}}],
    destination_path={{good_bin.path}},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{infeed_conveyor.path}}, {{gauge_probe.path}}, {{good_bin.path}}, {{reject_bin.path}}],
)
get_world_transform(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}})

    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{workpieces[1].path}}],
    destination_path={{inspect_fixture.path}},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{infeed_conveyor.path}}, {{gauge_probe.path}}, {{good_bin.path}}, {{reject_bin.path}}],
)
raycast(origin={{gauge_probe.raycast_origin}}, direction={{gauge_probe.raycast_direction}}, max_distance={{gauge_probe.max_distance}})

    destination_path={{reject_bin.path}},

)

    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{workpieces[2].path}}],
    destination_path={{inspect_fixture.path}},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{infeed_conveyor.path}}, {{gauge_probe.path}}, {{good_bin.path}}, {{reject_bin.path}}],
)

    source_paths=[{{workpieces[2].path}}],
